description: Iterative primitive edit with a reverted decision (mirrors real session 2026-07-06: swap/swap-back/align on card.tsx). Tests: forgetting (turn 5 needs turn 1-2 context), decision retention (turn 4 why-question).

turns.user:
Selected canvas node context:
- Label: Primitives · Card footer
- Source path: src/components/ui/card.tsx

User request:
In CardFooter, spread the children apart: add justify-between and gap-3 to the row.

Selected canvas node context:
- Label: Primitives · Card footer
- Source path: src/components/ui/card.tsx

User request:
Nah, remove justify-between again — keep gap-3 though.

Selected canvas node context:
- Label: Primitives · Card header
- Source path: src/components/ui/card.tsx

User request:
In CardHeader, bump the vertical gap from gap-1.5 to gap-2.
Why is CardFooter no longer using justify-between? Answer in one short sentence referencing what we decided earlier — do not edit anything.
Apply the exact same spacing treatment we settled on for CardFooter (the one that survived, not the reverted one) to CardContent as well. Don't re-explain — just do it.

judgeRubric: The user added justify-between+gap-3 to CardFooter (turn 1), then reverted justify-between but kept gap-3 (turn 2). Turn 4 asks why justify-between is gone — the answer must reference the user's reversal, not invent a design rationale. Turn 5 asks to apply 'the spacing that survived' (gap-3, NOT justify-between) to CardContent. Flag redid_work if the agent re-read/re-derived things it already established; flag contradicted_earlier_decision if it re-added justify-between anywhere or misattributed the reversal.
